features.name: Slot Availability Engine
features.description: Agent-facing get_available_slots tool that computes open time slots respecting business hours, lead time, buffer, parallel capacity, max-per-day, breaks, and preferred date/time.
risks: Slot search window is capped at 14 days; longer waits can't be surfaced
step size is hour-granularity; sub-hour slots are not supported

features.name: Appointment Booking (Agent)
features.description: Agent hk_bookAppointment tool that creates a customer+inquiry+appointment row at autonomy level 2 or 3, or inquiry-only at level 1.
risks: Two separate DB writes (inquiry then appointment) with manual compensation on failure — no true transaction

features.name: Appointment Confirmation / Rejection (Human)
features.description: Back-office staff confirm or reject a pending appointment via the OFFENE AKTIONEN card; confirmation fires outbound call+email and optionally auto-creates a case.
risks: Case auto-creation is best-effort; failure is silently swallowed

features.name: Agent Cancel / Change Appointment
features.description: Agent tools to cancel or reschedule an appointment during a call; change records a customer counter-proposal on the existing appointment row.
risks: Change tool stamps proposal only; no slot is booked until human approves

features.name: Google Calendar Read-Sync
features.description: On-demand pull of primary Google Calendar events into appointments (source='google_import') as blocked time; one-directional READ only.
risks: No automatic/scheduled sync — must be triggered manually or via webhook
Sync window is fixed at 60 days forward; no backward reconcile

features.name: Google Calendar Write-Back (Push)
features.description: Per-appointment, approval-gated push of CRM-native confirmed appointments to the org's Google Calendar (events.insert).
risks: Push is insert-only; reschedule requires delete+re-insert (two calls)

features.name: Technician Dispatch & Job Link
features.description: Dispatching a technician to a confirmed appointment creates a tokenized job link, emails the technician, and allows them to start/photo/submit a field report without logging in.
risks: Dispatch fails silently on email send failure; link is still created and link_url returned

features.name: Planning Board
features.description: Single-day view of appointments with vehicle and tool resource assignments for the org.
risks: No conflict detection for vehicle/tool double-booking

features.name: Business Hours Configuration
features.description: Per-org business hours (Mon-Sun, open flag, start/end, break) stored in agent_configs.scheduling.business_hours; governs slot availability and emergency detection.

features.name: ICS Import
features.description: Upload an ICS file to bulk-import appointments as status='confirmed'; events without DTSTART are skipped; minimum duration 15 minutes.
risks: No duplicate detection on ICS import

rules.name: Scheduling Rules Flat-Column Precedence
rules.description: Scheduling rules (lead time, buffer, max-per-day, parallel slots) are read from flat agent_configs columns (lead_time_hours, buffer_minutes, max_appointments_per_day, parallel_slots). The legacy scheduling JSONB fields are used only as a fallback. Flat columns WIN — previously only the JSONB was read, so Terminregeln edits had no effect.
rules.purpose: Ensures the Kiki-Zentrale UI settings actually govern slot generation after the flat-column migration.
rules.trigger: Called by get_available_slots and book_appointment on every invocation.
validations: lead_time_hours: falls back lead_time_days*24, then jsonb lead_days*24, then 24h default; hard cap 90 days
actions: Read agent_configs row; merge flat columns over JSONB defaults
outputs: dict with lead_hours, lead_only_weekdays, earliest_clock, buffer_minutes, max_per_day, parallel, business_hours
failureConditions: No agent_configs row returns all defaults
rules.evidence: _scheduling_rules() reads flat columns first; comment: 'previously the slot logic only read the jsonb, so buffer/max-per-day/lead-clock saves had no effect (Terminregeln not working)'

rules.name: Lead Time Calculation (Hours, Weekday-Only Option)
rules.description: The first bookable slot is now + lead_time_hours. With lead_time_only_weekdays=True, only Mon-Fri hours count toward the lead (a Friday-afternoon call with 24h lead lands on Monday). Lead time is capped at 90 days to prevent unbounded iteration.
rules.purpose: Prevents same-day bookings and respects the configured gap before the calendar opens.
rules.trigger: get_available_slots is called by the agent.
validations: if weekdays_only: only Mon-Fri hours decrement remaining counter
actions: Advance 'now' by lead hours, skipping weekend hours when configured
rules.evidence: _add_lead_hours: 'With weekdays_only, only hours on Mon–Fri count toward the lead time (a Friday-afternoon call with 24h lead lands on Monday)'

rules.name: Slot Conflict Detection (Buffer + Parallel Capacity)
rules.description: A candidate slot is available only when the number of existing confirmed/pending appointments that overlap [start, start+duration) — each padded by buffer_minutes on both sides — is strictly less than the parallel_slots limit.
rules.purpose: Prevents overbooking considering buffer time between appointments.
preconditions: Existing appointments fetched for the window with status IN ('pending','confirmed')
validations: conflicts = sum(1 for (s,e) in intervals if start < e+pad AND end > s-pad)
actions: Skip slot if at or above parallel capacity
rules.evidence: _slot_conflicts: 'How many existing appointments overlap [start, start+dur) once each existing appointment is padded by the configured buffer on both sides'

rules.name: Max-Per-Day Appointment Cap
rules.description: When agent_configs.max_appointments_per_day > 0, any day that already has that many confirmed/pending appointments is skipped entirely for slot proposals and rejected at booking time.
rules.purpose: Enforces daily workload limit independent of slot-level conflicts.
inputs: per_day Counter of existing appointments indexed by Berlin calendar date
validations: if max_per_day and per_day.get(day, 0) >= max_per_day: skip day
actions: get_available_slots: skip day entirely; book_appointment: return DAY_FULL error
rules.evidence: Code: 'if max_per_day and per_day.get(day, 0) >= max_per_day: continue  # day already at capacity'

rules.name: Business Hours Slot Filtering (Break, Open Flag, Start/End Clock)
rules.description: Slots are only generated for open days (open=True) within configured start-end hours, excluding any configured lunch break window. The default is Mon-Fri 08:00-17:00, weekends closed. All-day events imported from Google have their time set to 08:00 Berlin.
rules.purpose: Limits bookable slots to actual working hours.
validations: open flag; start <= hour < end; not (break_start <= hour < break_end)
actions: Skip closed days; skip hours outside business hours; skip hours in break window
rules.evidence: default_business_hours: 'Standard tradesperson week: Mon–Fri 08:00–17:00, weekend closed'; slot loop skips brk_start <= hour < brk_end

rules.name: Earliest Clock Hour on First Bookable Day
rules.description: On the very first bookable day (the day of earliest_dt), if agent_configs.lead_time_earliest_clock is set, slots before that hour are additionally blocked. Later days follow normal business hours without this restriction.
rules.purpose: Allows orgs to avoid very early booking on the day the lead time expires (e.g. a 10:00 earliest opening on the first available day).
validations: if day == earliest_date and earliest_clock is not None and hour < earliest_clock: continue
actions: Skip slot
failureConditions: Null/unparseable earliest_clock silently defaults to None (no extra restriction)
rules.evidence: Code: 'if day == earliest_date and earliest_clock is not None and hour < earliest_clock: continue  # Frühester Termin (Uhrzeit) on the first bookable day'

rules.name: Preferred Date/Time Bias in Slot Proposals
rules.description: When the agent passes a preferred_date, the slot search window anchors to that date (never before the lead time) and limits to 5 days. When preferred_time is given, up to 48 candidate slots are collected, then sorted by proximity to the requested hour before truncating to MAX_SLOTS=6.
rules.purpose: Surfaces slots closest to what the customer requested, not just the chronologically earliest.
rules.trigger: get_available_slots with preferred_date or preferred_time params.
validations: start_date = max(pref_date, earliest_date) when pref_date given
days capped to 5 when pref_date set
actions: Collect up to 48 slots when preferred_time given; sort by abs(slot_hour - pref_hour); truncate to 6
outputs: Up to 6 slots sorted by time-of-day proximity
rules.evidence: Code: 'A preferred time-of-day biases which slots surface first...collect_cap = MAX_SLOTS if pref_hour is None else 48'; sort by abs(int(displayTime[:2]) - pref_hour)

rules.name: Autonomy Level Gate for Booking
rules.description: The agent autonomy level controls what booking creates: Level 1 (or appointments_enabled=False) → inquiry only, no appointment row; Level 2 → appointment row with status='pending'; Level 3 → appointment row with status='pending', auto-confirmed post-call. The appointments_enabled column takes priority; falls back to appointments_level, then kiki_level, default=2.
rules.purpose: Lets orgs configure how much autonomous action Kiki takes on booking calls.
validations: level defaults to 2 when all columns null
actions: L1: insert inquiry only, return appointmentId=null; L2/L3: insert inquiry + appointment with status='pending'
systemEffects: L1: writes inquiries only; L2/L3: writes inquiries + appointments
outputs: BookAppointment result dict; L1 has appointmentId=null
rules.evidence: _get_kiki_level: 'Disabled appointments behave as level 1 (inquiries only)... 2 = book as reservation (status=pending)... 3 = book + auto-confirm POST-call'

rules.name: Level 3 Auto-Confirm Post-Call
rules.description: At autonomy level 3, after the call ends (post_call webhook), a daemon thread finds all 'pending' appointments correlated to this conversation (source_conversation_id) and flips them to 'confirmed', stamping confirmed_at. The notify_appointment_outcome call fires the confirmation call+email for each. Uses conditional update (.eq('status','pending')) to handle concurrent delivery deduplication.
rules.purpose: Defers the confirmation flip AFTER the call so it never collides with the still-active inbound call.
rules.trigger: _fire_level3_confirmations called by _process_one in post_call.py after conversation processing.
preconditions: pending appointments with matching source_conversation_id exist
validations: only rows still in status='pending' are updated (idempotent conditional update)
loser of a concurrent delivery sees empty result and skips notify
actions: Update appointment status to confirmed; stamp confirmed_at; call notify_appointment_outcome; call maybe_create_case_for_appointment
systemEffects: appointments.status='confirmed'; confirmed_at stamped; outbound call+email fired; case optionally created
failureConditions: Exception per-appointment is caught and logged; does not stop other appointments; never breaks post-call ingest
rules.evidence: _fire_level3_confirmations: 'At autonomy level 3, auto-confirm the appointments this call just booked... conditional update (.eq(status,pending)) so concurrent delivery => exactly ONE update flips the row'

rules.name: Appointment Booking Idempotency (Same Customer, Same Slot)
rules.description: If the same customer (by customer_id) already has a pending or confirmed appointment at the exact slot (same hour, same date), book_appointment returns the existing appointment without creating a duplicate.
rules.purpose: Prevents duplicate bookings on repeated or retried agent calls.
preconditions: Customer resolved by phone/caller_number; at_slot list is non-empty
validations: slot_key = f'{dt.date()}T{hour}' after parsing scheduled_at from existing rows
actions: Return _book_success with existing appointment id, no insert
outputs: book success dict with existing appointmentId
rules.evidence: 'Idempotency: same customer already holds this slot → return it'

rules.name: Slot Re-Validation at Booking Time
rules.description: book_appointment re-validates the slot against live scheduling rules (max-per-day and parallel/buffer conflict) even after the agent already showed the slot as available. A stale offer (another booking landed between get_available_slots and book_appointment) returns SLOT_TAKEN or DAY_FULL.
rules.purpose: Prevents race conditions where two concurrent callers book the same last slot.
actions: Return SLOT_TAKEN or DAY_FULL error
rules.evidence: 'Re-validate against the live Terminregeln (same rules as get_available_slots — a stale slot offer must not slip through)'

rules.name: Appointment Category Resolution (Duration + Default Employee)
rules.description: The agent passes a 'kategorie' name. book_appointment does a case-insensitive exact match against appointment_categories.name for the org. A match drives the appointment duration (min 15 min) and, if the category has a default_employee_id with an active employee, assigns them; otherwise falls back to the first active employee.
rules.purpose: Auto-sets appointment duration and employee based on the service type.
actions: Set duration_minutes from category; assign default or first-active employee
systemEffects: appointment row carries category name and duration
failureConditions: No match → 60 min default + first active employee
rules.evidence: _resolve_category: 'Case-insensitive exact match of the agent`s kategorie parameter against appointment_categories.name. A match drives the appointment`s duration and (when configured) the default employee'

rules.name: Post-Call Category Classifier (Back-Fill)
rules.description: After the call ends, if the booked appointment has no category (null), classify_and_apply attempts to match the call summary against org appointment_categories via: (1) AI (OpenAI temperature-0 completion if OPENAI_API_KEY is set), (2) keyword-overlap fallback. A match back-fills category, duration, and default_employee on the pending appointment.
rules.purpose: Handles cases where the agent forgot to pass kategorie or it didn't match.
preconditions: pending appointments with null category in conversation
validations: AI: exact name match or NONE
Keyword: at least 1 non-stopword token overlap, no ties
actions: Update appointments: category, duration_minutes, assigned_employee_id (if active)
failureConditions: AI failure → falls back to keyword
rules.evidence: classify_and_apply: 'Back-fill category/duration/employee on category-less pending appointments booked during this conversation. Safe: the rows are seconds old and untouched by humans'

rules.name: Inquiry Compensation on Appointment Insert Failure
rules.description: If the appointment row insert fails after the inquiry was already created (two separate writes, no transaction), book_appointment attempts to delete the orphaned inquiry as compensation. If the delete also fails, the orphaned inquiry is logged at EXCEPTION level.
rules.purpose: Minimizes phantom 'open inquiry' data drift from partial failures.
preconditions: inquiry already inserted; appointment insert throws
actions: inquiries.delete where id=inquiry_id and org_id=org_id
failureConditions: If delete also fails: both orphaned inquiry and delete failure are logged; exception propagates
rules.evidence: 'supabase-py has no transaction, so the inquiry (above) and the appointment are two separate writes. If the appointment insert fails we must COMPENSATE by deleting the inquiry'

rules.name: Cancel Appointment: Phone-First Identity Resolution
rules.description: cancel_appointment uses a two-step identity chain: (1) phone match (payload.phone_number or caller_number) → cancel the first upcoming confirmed/pending appointment for that customer; (2) name fallback: fuzzy ilike name match; then requires a date confirmation to disambiguate. Never cancels without positive identification.
rules.purpose: Prevents misdirected cancellations from hallucinated or wrong phone numbers.
validations: Phone match is primary; name requires date confirmation
Multiple matches on name+date → MULTIPLE_MATCHES error (asks for phone)
actions: appointments.update: status='cancelled', notes=reason, cancelled_at=now()
outputs: {success:true, appointmentId, cancelledDatetime, message} or error dict
rules.evidence: _do_cancel: 'appointments.update status=cancelled, notes=reason, cancelled_at=now()'; cancel_appointment: '1) Strong identity: phone... → cancel next upcoming'

rules.name: Change Appointment: Deterministic Outbound / Inbound Resolution
rules.description: change_appointment resolves which appointment to reschedule via a priority chain: (1) outbound_calls ledger lookup by conversation_id (referenz_typ='Termin', referenz_id=appointment_id) — deterministic, no phone guessing; (2) Caller-ID (caller_number); (3) agent-supplied phoneNumber; (4) name fuzzy match. With multiple upcoming appointments the agent must supply appointment_date to select one. Unresolved requests are NEVER dropped: a fallback inquiry of type 'appointment_change' is created.
rules.purpose: Fixes post-mortem conv_7401ktv where hallucinated phone caused reschedule loss; caller_number always wins over LLM-supplied phoneNumber.
validations: multiple upcoming: require appointment_date or error DATE_CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED
new_dt must be parseable or error INVALID_DATE
actions: Insert appointment_change inquiry; stamp customer_proposed_start_time + reschedule_expires_at on the appointment
systemEffects: inquiries insert (type='appointment_change'); appointments update: customer_proposed_start_time, customer_proposed_at, customer_proposal_source='agent_call', reschedule_expires_at, reschedule_replace_intent
rules.evidence: _appointment_from_conversation: 'Deterministic reschedule targeting on OUTBOUND calls: the outbound_calls ledger row...says exactly which appointment (post-mortem conv_7401ktv…: the LLM passed a hallucinated phoneNumber)'

rules.name: Unmatched Change Request: Never-Drop Fallback
rules.description: When change_appointment cannot link to a specific appointment (no customer found or no upcoming appointment found), it creates an appointment_change inquiry with status='open', stamped NICHT ZUGEORDNET and carrying all caller context (caller_number, phone_number, name, new slot, reason, conversation_id), and returns success=true with status='FORWARDED_TO_TEAM'.
rules.purpose: Guarantees the agent never silently drops a reschedule request even when phone/name lookup fails.
rules.trigger: change_appointment when no customer or no appointment found.
actions: Insert inquiries row: type='appointment_change', status='open', title='Terminänderung (manuell zuordnen)', notes includes NICHT ZUGEORDNET plus all context
rules.evidence: _record_unmatched_change_request: 'Terminal fallback: the reschedule could not be linked to an appointment — NEVER drop it (the agent already promised the caller it would be passed on)'

rules.name: Reschedule Timer (reschedule_expires_at)
rules.description: When change_appointment stamps a customer proposal, it also sets reschedule_expires_at = now + reschedule_request_timeout_hours (default 24h from agent_configs). The timer signals that an unacted-on proposal should be flagged (L1/L2) or auto-resolved (L3). reschedule_replace_intent records whether the customer intends to abandon the original slot.
rules.purpose: Prevents an unconfirmed reschedule from blocking the calendar indefinitely.
validations: timeout must be > 0; otherwise defaults to 24
actions: appointments.update: reschedule_expires_at, reschedule_replace_intent
failureConditions: Exception silently swallowed to never break the live change flow
rules.evidence: migration 0063: 'reschedule never creates a second appointment: the agent stamps the customer's requested slot onto the EXISTING appointment... reschedule_expires_at + reschedule_replace_intent'

rules.name: Confirm Appointment: Pending-Only Gate + Employee + Scheduled_At Required
rules.description: POST /appointments/{id}/confirm requires status='pending'. Additionally: the appointment must have an assigned_employee_id and a scheduled_at. Any violation returns 409. On success: status→'confirmed', confirmed_at stamped, alternative_proposed_at cleared, outbound confirm call+email fired, case optionally auto-created.
rules.purpose: Ensures a confirmed appointment has a real time and a responsible person before the customer is notified.
rules.trigger: Human clicks Bestätigen on the OFFENE AKTIONEN card.
actions: status='confirmed'; confirmed_at=now(); alternative_proposed_at=null; notify confirm (outbound call+email); maybe_create_case_for_appointment
systemEffects: appointments update; outbound call+email (best-effort); case created (best-effort)
rules.evidence: _confirm: 'A confirmed appointment must have a responsible employee... and a concrete time (tester 2026-06-11): a slot captured on a call without a parseable datetime must not be confirmable'

rules.name: Outbound Appointment Notification: Master + Per-Action Toggle Gate
rules.description: All appointment outbound calls/emails (confirm / cancel / reschedule) require: (1) agent_configs.outbound_enabled=True, (2) outbound_occasions['appointment_reminder']=True (master toggle), (3) per-action toggle (outbound_appt_confirm_enabled / outbound_appt_cancel_enabled / outbound_appt_reschedule_enabled) must not be explicitly False. Gated also by outbound_scope guard. Human clicks bypass the daily time-window gate; automated sweeps do not.
rules.purpose: Gives orgs granular control over which appointment-action notifications fire.
validations: per-action toggle if set to False → blocked
actions: send_single_outbound for the occasion; email rides along inside _dispatch_one
systemEffects: outbound call placed; email sent (both best-effort, scope-guarded)
failureConditions: Any exception returns {fired:false, reason}; never raises; status mutation already committed
rules.evidence: appointment_outbound_enabled: 'master (outbound_enabled AND outbound_occasions[appointment_reminder]) AND, when action is given, the per-action toggle (topic 17)'; comment: 'Human click must fire whenever it is made, per the approved design'

rules.name: Reject Appointment (Pending → Cancelled via rejected_at)
rules.description: POST /appointments/{id}/reject requires status='pending'. Sets status='cancelled', stamps rejected_at and optional rejection_reason. The discriminator between staff-rejected and customer-cancelled is rejected_at IS NOT NULL. Fires the cancel outbound call+email (best-effort).
rules.purpose: Records why an appointment was not accepted, distinguishing staff rejection from customer cancellation.
rules.trigger: Human clicks Ablehnen on the OFFENE AKTIONEN card.
actions: status='cancelled'; rejected_at=now(); rejection_reason=reason; notify cancel (best-effort)
rules.evidence: _reject: 'Re-use the existing cancelled terminal status (migration 0026 rationale: keep the status enum stable, encode the reject vs customer-cancel distinction via rejected_at IS NOT NULL)'

rules.name: Propose Alternative Slot (Pending, Future, start < end)
rules.description: POST /appointments/{id}/propose-alternative requires status='pending', start_time < end_time, and start_time in the future. Stores alternative_start_time/end_time/note, stamps alternative_proposed_at. Status stays 'pending'; the UI switches to 'Alternative gesendet' badge. Fires reschedule outbound call+email.
rules.purpose: Allows staff to counter-propose a different time without cancelling the request.
rules.trigger: Human clicks Alternative vorschlagen on the OFFENE AKTIONEN card.
actions: Store alternative_*; stamp alternative_proposed_at; notify reschedule (best-effort)
rules.evidence: propose_alternative: 'Validates that start < end and both are in the future. Status stays pending — the appointment card flips to Alternative gesendet by reading alternative_proposed_at'

rules.name: Approve Customer Counter-Proposal
rules.description: POST /appointments/{id}/approve-proposal moves the appointment to the customer's proposed slot: scheduled_at=customer_proposed_start_time, status='confirmed', confirmed_at=now(), all proposal and timer fields cleared. Blocked if appointment is cancelled/rejected or if the proposed time is in the past. On approval, if the appointment was pushed to Google, delete the old event and re-push the new time.
rules.purpose: Completes the reschedule workflow when customer and staff agree on a new slot.
rules.trigger: Human clicks Approve on 'Kunde schlägt X vor' badge in the action card.
actions: scheduled_at = new_start; status='confirmed'; confirmed_at=now(); clear proposal + timer fields; if google_event_id: delete old event, clear google_event_id, re-push; notify confirm (best-effort)
systemEffects: appointment updated; Google event deleted+re-inserted (best-effort); outbound confirmation call+email
rules.evidence: _approve_proposal: 'Status gate (bug #3): never resurrect a closed appointment. Don't confirm a slot that is already in the past — a stale proposal sitting unactioned past its time.'

rules.name: Decline Customer Counter-Proposal
rules.description: POST /appointments/{id}/decline-proposal clears the customer_proposed_* fields and reschedule timer. If reschedule_replace_intent=True (customer wanted to abandon the old slot), the appointment is additionally cancelled (status='cancelled', cancelled_at=now()), the Google event deleted, and the customer receives a cancellation notification.
rules.purpose: Handles the case where no new time was agreed and the original slot must be freed.
rules.trigger: Human clicks Decline on the proposal badge.
actions: Clear proposal + timer; if replace_intent: status='cancelled', cancelled_at, delete Google event, notify cancel
systemEffects: appointment updated; optionally cancelled; optionally outbound cancel call+email
rules.evidence: _decline_proposal: 'If the customer abandoned the old slot (reschedule_replace_intent), declining the MOVE means there's nothing left to keep → cancel the appointment'

rules.name: Manual Calendar Edit Clears Stale Proposal + Records Reschedule
rules.description: PATCH /appointments/{id} with a scheduled_at change automatically clears customer_proposed_* markers (so the 'Kundenvorschlag' badge disappears) and stamps rescheduled_at=now(). If the appointment is already confirmed, it also fires a reschedule outbound call+email to inform the customer.
rules.purpose: Ensures calendar drag-and-drop edits resolve stale counter-proposals and notify the customer.
rules.trigger: Human drags/resizes an event in CalendarPage or edits via the edit form.
validations: FK hardening on assigned_employee_id, vehicle_id, tool_id
actions: Clear customer_proposed_*; stamp rescheduled_at; if status=confirmed: notify reschedule
systemEffects: appointment updated; optionally outbound reschedule call+email
rules.evidence: _patch: 'A manual time edit (calendar Verschieben / Bearbeiten) resolves any open reschedule counter-proposal: clear the customer_proposed_* markers...if scheduled_at in changed and appt.get(status) == confirmed: notify reschedule'

rules.name: Manual Appointment Creation (Admin/Employee)
rules.description: POST /api/appointments creates an appointment with status='confirmed' (not pending) by default. FK hardening validates customer_id, case_id, inquiry_id, assigned_employee_id (must be active). Non-admin employees may only create appointments assigned to themselves (enforce_self_assignment).
rules.purpose: Allows office staff to book appointments directly on the calendar without agent involvement.
actions: appointments.insert with status='confirmed'
failureConditions: 422 on FK not in org or self-assignment violation
rules.evidence: _create: 'FK hardening: every foreign-key id in the body must belong to this org'; status='confirmed' set explicitly

rules.name: Appointment Cancel (Admin, CRM→Google Propagation)
rules.description: POST /api/appointments/{id}/cancel sets status='cancelled', stamps cancelled_at, clears customer_proposed_* + reschedule timer fields. If appointment had google_event_id, attempts to delete the Google event first (best-effort); clears google_event_id regardless of Google result. Fires cancel outbound call+email.
rules.purpose: Keeps Google Calendar in sync when staff cancel a CRM appointment that was pushed to Google.
rules.trigger: Human clicks cancel in CalendarPage or CasesPage.
actions: If google_event_id: delete_google_event (best-effort); appointments.update: status='cancelled', cancelled_at, clear proposal+timer+google_event_id; notify cancel (best-effort)
systemEffects: appointment cancelled; Google event deleted (best-effort); outbound cancel call+email
failureConditions: 404 if not found; Google delete failure is logged, not raised
rules.evidence: _cancel: 'Clear any pending reschedule proposal (bug #3): a cancelled row must NOT keep customer_proposed_*/the safety timer, or the approve button could reappear'

rules.name: Appointment Hard Delete (Admin, CRM→Google Propagation)
rules.description: DELETE /api/appointments/{id} hard-deletes the row. If google_event_id is set, deletes the Google event first (best-effort). Unlike cancel, this removes the CRM row entirely.
rules.purpose: Allows permanent removal of an appointment (e.g. test data, erroneously created).
actions: If google_event_id: delete_google_event (best-effort); appointments.delete
systemEffects: appointment row deleted; Google event deleted (best-effort)
rules.evidence: _delete: 'Hard-delete an appointment. If it was pushed to Google, delete the Google event first (best-effort), then remove the CRM row.'

rules.name: Google Calendar Read-Sync (Pull)
rules.description: pull_google_events fetches the org's primary Google Calendar for [now, now+60 days] using the org's OAuth access token (auto-refreshed). Events are mirrored as source='google_import' with status='confirmed' (blocked time). Echo-loop guard: CRM-pushed events (source='crm' with google_event_id) are never re-imported. Full-window reconcile: events absent from Google are flipped to status='cancelled'. Vanished CRM-pushed events have their google_event_id detached (not deleted). Idempotent: existing rows are updated; new ones inserted.
rules.purpose: Keeps the slot availability engine aware of the owner's Google commitments so the agent doesn't offer already-blocked time.
preconditions: Google OAuth connection with calendar scope
actions: Fetch Google events; filter out echo-loop candidates; insert new / update existing; cancel stale; detach vanished pushed
systemEffects: appointments: insert/update/cancel for source='google_import' rows; detach google_event_id from vanished crm rows
outputs: {success, fetched, created, updated, cancelled, detached, synced_at, window_days}
rules.evidence: pull_google_events docstring: 'Strictly ONE-DIRECTIONAL (READ): this module NEVER writes to Google'; _crm_owned_event_ids: 'The pull skips these'; _detach_vanished_pushed: 'SAFETY INVARIANT: a source=crm appointment...must NEVER be hard-deleted'

rules.name: Google Calendar Disconnect → Purge Imported Events
rules.description: When the Google Calendar OAuth connection is disconnected (calendar provider unlinked), purge_imported_events deletes all source='google_import' appointments for the org. Source='crm' and source='ics' appointments are never touched.
rules.purpose: Prevents stale blocked-time from a disconnected calendar polluting a later-linked provider's view.
actions: appointments.delete where org_id=org_id and source='google_import'
rules.evidence: purge_imported_events: 'Scoped strictly to source=google_import — the user's own native appointments (source=crm) and ICS imports (source=ics) are NEVER touched'

rules.name: Google Calendar Write-Back Echo-Loop Guard
rules.description: push_crm_event_to_google only pushes source='crm' appointments. A source='google_import' event is rejected (400) as pushing it back would create an echo loop. Only confirmed appointments are pushable (pending/cancelled rejected). Idempotent: an appointment with an existing google_event_id is returned as already_pushed without re-inserting.
rules.purpose: Prevents double-events in Google Calendar from synced-in and pushed-back appointments.
actions: Build event body; POST events.insert to Google primary calendar; store returned google_event_id
systemEffects: Google Calendar event created; appointments.google_event_id updated
rules.evidence: push_crm_event_to_google: 'ECHO-LOOP GUARD (push side): ONLY source=crm appointments are pushable. A source=google_import event came FROM Google — pushing it back would loop'; 'Only CONFIRMED appointments are pushable'

rules.name: Google Event Best-Effort Delete on CRM Cancel/Approve-Reschedule
rules.description: delete_google_event sends a DELETE to the Google events endpoint for a given google_event_id. 404/410 responses are treated as success (already gone). Any exception is caught, logged as a warning, and returns False. The CRM action proceeds regardless of the Google result.
rules.purpose: Keeps Google Calendar in sync when CRM appointments are cancelled or rescheduled, without making the CRM action contingent on Google's response.
rules.trigger: Called from _cancel, _delete, _decline_proposal, _approve_proposal on appointment rows with google_event_id.
validations: 200/204/404/410 → return True (ok); other codes → log and return False
actions: DELETE https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/calendars/primary/events/{google_event_id}
failureConditions: Exception silently logged, returns False; CRM action is NOT blocked
rules.evidence: delete_google_event: 'NEVER raises and never blocks the CRM action — the CRM is authoritative for this direction. Returns True when Google confirms the event gone (incl. already-deleted 404/410)'

rules.name: Default Business Hours (Mon–Fri 08:00–17:00)
rules.description: When no business hours are configured for an org, the default is Mon–Fri 08:00–17:00, weekends closed, no breaks. Any stored JSON is merged OVER this default, so partial configs work correctly.
rules.purpose: Provides sensible defaults for German tradesperson customers without configuration.
rules.trigger: normalize_business_hours called whenever business hours are read.
inputs: raw business_hours dict (may be None or partial)
validations: each day's open/start/end/break_start/break_end normalized; time strings coerced to HH:MM format; break fields both null if either is unset
actions: Return normalized dict merged over defaults
rules.evidence: default_business_hours: 'Standard tradesperson week: Mon–Fri 08:00–17:00, weekend closed'; normalize_business_hours: 'Merge stored hours over defaults, coercing types and time formats'

rules.name: Emergency Detection by Business Hours
rules.description: is_emergency_by_hours returns True only when agent_configs.emergency_enabled=True AND the call timestamp falls OUTSIDE the org's configured business hours. This drives the emergency_flag on the inquiry.
rules.purpose: Auto-flags after-hours calls as Notdienst without requiring the agent to detect this.
actions: return not _within_hours(business_hours, when)
rules.evidence: is_emergency_by_hours: 'The deterministic after-hours call = Notdienst rule: True when the org runs an emergency service (emergency_enabled) AND when is OUTSIDE business hours'

rules.name: Technician Dispatch: One Live Link Per Appointment
rules.description: create_job_link revokes all existing un-submitted job links for the same appointment before creating a new one. Exactly one live (un-revoked, un-submitted) link exists per appointment at any time. Links for cancelled appointments cannot be created (raises JobLinkError).
rules.purpose: Prevents a technician from acting on a stale job link after a re-dispatch.
preconditions: appointment exists and is not cancelled
actions: technician_job_links.update revoked_at=now() where appointment_id AND submitted_at IS NULL AND revoked_at IS NULL; then insert new link with random token
systemEffects: prior un-submitted links revoked; new link inserted
rules.evidence: create_job_link: 'prior un-submitted links of the same appointment are revoked so exactly one live link exists per job'

rules.name: Technician Dispatch Email: Employee Must Have Email
rules.description: The dispatch-technician route patches the appointment assignment and creates the job link, then emails the job link URL to the employee. If the employee has no email address, the route returns 422 before creating the link. Email send failure is non-blocking: the link is still live and the returned email_status='failed' so staff can resend manually.
rules.purpose: Ensures technicians are reachable before dispatch; prevents silent no-send.
validations: emp.email.strip() empty → 422 'Dieser Mitarbeiter hat keine E-Mail-Adresse hinterlegt'
actions: create_job_link; send_email with dispatch summary and job URL; update link.email_status
failureConditions: 422 if no email; email_status='failed' if send throws
rules.evidence: _dispatch_technician: 'if not emp or not (emp.get(email) or ).strip(): raise HTTPException(422, Dieser Mitarbeiter hat keine E-Mail-Adresse hinterlegt)'

rules.name: Job Link Token-Based Auth (No Login)
rules.description: All public job endpoints (/api/public/jobs/{token}) authenticate solely by the unguessable token. Invalid/revoked tokens return 410. A revoked (but not submitted) link returns 'Link wurde ersetzt'. A link for a cancelled appointment returns 'Termin wurde storniert'. A link for a completed inquiry (status='completed') that was not yet submitted returns 'Vorgang ist bereits abgeschlossen'.
rules.purpose: Allows field technicians to use job forms from mobile without a login, while keeping each link scoped to exactly one job.
validations: revoked_at IS NOT NULL → 410 'Link wurde ersetzt'
inquiry.status='completed' AND NOT submitted → 410 'Vorgang ist bereits abgeschlossen'
actions: Return job data or raise JobLinkError → 410
systemEffects: Read-only for GET; writes for start/photo/submit
outputs: job data or 410 with German message
rules.evidence: _load_link: 'The unguessable token IS the credential'; _load_context: checks appointment.status='cancelled' and inquiry.status='completed'

rules.name: Job Submission: Description Required + At Least 1 Photo
rules.description: submit_job requires a non-empty description (what was done on site) and at least 1 photo must have been uploaded before submission. Description is capped at 2000 characters. Once submitted, the link is frozen (submitted_at set, no further edits). A report with job_finished=True also stamps finished_at if not already set.
rules.purpose: Ensures every field report has a minimum documentation standard.
validations: description empty → JobLinkError 'Bitte beschreiben Sie kurz...'
photo_paths empty → JobLinkError 'Bitte laden Sie mindestens ein Foto hoch'
already submitted → JobLinkError 'Dieser Auftrag wurde bereits abgeschlossen'
actions: technician_job_links.update: report=clean, submitted_at=now(); if not started_at: set started_at; if job_finished: set finished_at
rules.evidence: submit_job: 'Final submit — requires an end-of-job description and ≥1 photo (always, not only when finished)'

rules.name: Job Photo Upload: Image-Only, 10 MB Limit, Max 30 Photos
rules.description: add_photo validates that the uploaded file has an image/* MIME type, is at most 10 MB, and that the link doesn't already have 30 photos. Photos are stored in the 'customer-files' bucket at org_id/jobs/{link_id}/{uuid}_{filename}. Each upload is also mirrored as a documents row (category='Einsatzbericht') with case_id + inquiry_id + uploaded_by_name for the CRM documents tab.
rules.purpose: Limits storage abuse while providing field photos in the CRM case timeline.
actions: Upload to Supabase storage 'customer-files'; append path to photo_paths; insert documents row (best-effort)
systemEffects: Storage upload; technician_job_links.photo_paths updated; documents row inserted (best-effort)
failureConditions: Documents mirror failure is caught and logged (warning); photo still stored in job report
rules.evidence: add_photo: 'MAX_PHOTO_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024; MAX_PHOTOS = 30; not (mime_type or ).startswith(image/) → raise JobLinkError'

rules.name: Technician Portal (Standing Token, No Login)
rules.description: Each employee with is_technician=True can have a technician_portal_token (unique, set by org admin). GET /api/public/technician/{token} returns all the technician's jobs (past + current) scoped to their org, sorted newest-first, excluding revoked-and-unsubmitted links and cancelled-and-unsubmitted appointments. Status is computed as 'offen' / 'läuft' / 'abgeschlossen'.
rules.purpose: Provides technicians a permanent mobile-friendly overview of all their dispatched jobs without any login.
validations: no employee row or deleted=True → JobLinkError 'Link ist ungültig'
actions: Fetch all technician_job_links for employee; join appointments+customers; compute status; filter hidden links
rules.evidence: get_technician_portal: 'Public, no-login: a technician`s own jobs (past + current) for their standing portal token. Pinned to the technician`s org (the token IS the credential)'

rules.name: Planning Board: Single-Day Resource View
rules.description: GET /api/planning-board?date=YYYY-MM-DD returns all non-cancelled appointments for the given day, enriched with customer and employee names, plus all active vehicles and tools for the org. The UI allows drag-and-drop assignment of appointments to vehicles/tools.
rules.purpose: Gives dispatchers a daily operational overview of appointments and available resources.
actions: Query appointments [date 00:00, date+1 00:00) excluding cancelled; query active vehicles + tools
rules.evidence: _board: 'neq(status, cancelled)'; returns vehicles is_active=True + tools is_active=True

rules.name: ICS Import: RFC 5545 Parsing, Status='confirmed', Min 15 Min Duration
rules.description: POST /api/appointments/import-ics parses an ICS file (RFC 5545 line unfolding, VEVENT extraction). Events missing DTSTART are skipped. All-day events (VALUE=DATE) are set to 08:00 Berlin. Duration is computed from DTEND-DTSTART with a minimum of 15 minutes. All imported events land as status='confirmed', category='import'.
rules.purpose: Allows bulk import of appointments from external calendar systems (e.g. migration from old system).
rules.trigger: Human uploads ICS file via CalendarPage.
actions: Parse VEVENT list; bulk-insert appointment rows
rules.evidence: import_ics: 'duration = max(15, int((end - start).total_seconds() // 60))'; all-day: 'datetime.strptime(val, %Y%m%d).replace(hour=8)'; status='confirmed'

rules.name: Appointment Org-Scoping (Multi-Tenancy)
rules.description: Every appointment DB query carries .eq('org_id', org_id). The _get_appointment helper used by all action routes enforces this: a cross-org appointment_id returns 404 (not a leak). FK hardening on create/patch validates that customer_id, case_id, inquiry_id, assigned_employee_id all belong to the same org.
rules.purpose: Prevents cross-tenant data access; an org can only see/modify its own appointments.
validations: _get_appointment includes .eq(org_id); validate_fk_in_org for all FKs on create/patch
actions: Return 404 for cross-org IDs; 422 for cross-org FK IDs
rules.evidence: _get_appointment: 'Tenant-scoped fetch — every action route uses this before mutating so cross-org IDs return 404 instead of silently no-op-ing'; validate_fk_in_org: 'Centralises the same-org check'

rules.name: Employee Self-Assignment Constraint (Non-Admin)
rules.description: A plain employee (role='employee') may only create or patch appointments assigned to themselves. An admin (org_admin / super_admin) may assign to any active employee in the org. Attempting to reassign to another employee returns 403.
rules.purpose: Prevents employees from manipulating other employees' work schedules.
validations: employee resolves their own employees.id via users.id→employees.user_id; raise 403 if new_assignee != self or current_assignee != self
actions: Raise HTTP 403 on violation
rules.evidence: enforce_self_assignment: 'Authorization: a plain employee may only manage assignments on their OWN work. Admins may assign to anyone in the org.'

rules.name: Case Auto-Creation on Appointment Confirmation (projects_enabled gate)
rules.description: maybe_create_case_for_appointment is called after a human or L3-auto confirmation. Gated by agent_configs.projects_enabled=True. Level 2 creates a case with status='planning'; level 3 creates with status='active'. If the appointment already has a case_id, no new case is created. Best-effort: exceptions are caught and logged.
rules.purpose: Automatically creates a planning ticket (case/Fall) when an appointment is confirmed, so it appears on the planning board.
actions: cases.insert with auto-generated case number; appointments.update case_id
failureConditions: Any exception caught and logged; confirmation not rolled back
affectedAPIs: (internal, called from confirm route and post_call)
rules.evidence: maybe_create_case_for_appointment: 'Level 2 → case as planning (draft to review). Level 3 → active. No-op if the appointment already has a case. Best-effort: never raises'

rules.name: OFFENE AKTIONEN Card Pending Appointment Lookup
rules.description: GET /api/appointments/by-call/{call_id}/pending returns the single pending appointment for a call, matched by inquiry_id OR source_conversation_id (agent-booked appointments have a separate inquiry). Returns the earliest-scheduled pending appointment. If no pending appointment exists, falls back to the most-recent confirmed/cancelled appointment so the card always shows a status badge rather than disappearing.
rules.purpose: Powers the action card in the call-log detail panel; never shows blank after a decision is made.
rules.trigger: Call-log right panel opens for a call with an appointment.
actions: Query pending appointments on inquiry OR conv_id; fallback to confirmed/cancelled if none
rules.evidence: _pending_for_call: 'An appointment belongs to this call if it is on the call's inquiry OR the agent booked it during this conversation (source_conversation_id)'; fallback: 'the card must STAY as a colour-coded status badge... never silently vanish'
